year_1.label: Year 1 — Foundational Coursework and Clinic Entry

year_3.label: Year 3 — Advanced Assessment, Specialization, and Dissertation Prep

electives.description: One to two clinical electives from departmental offerings

year_5.label: Year 5 — APA Internship
year_5.note: Full-time APA-accredited predoctoral internship (2,000 hours). Students must be advanced to candidacy before beginning internship.

clinical_electives.description: Students choose from a variety of advanced clinical courses. Sample options include:

dissertation_requirement.description: Original empirical dissertation required. Students enroll in PPSY700 (Prep), then PPSY701/702 (continuation), and PPSY703 (1-credit maintenance) until defense.

practicum_sequence.description: Practicum begins Year 1 at the Center for Psychological Services (CPS) and affiliated community sites. Practica increase in complexity across years.

constraints: APA-accredited program; students must meet APA Standards of Accreditation guidelines.
Minimum 2,000 predoctoral internship hours required (CLPSY657 and CLPSY658).
Students must be admitted to doctoral candidacy (comprehensive exams passed) before starting internship.
Clinic practicum begins in Year 1; students must maintain placement hours each semester.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
All required coursework must be completed before dissertation defense.
Dissertation committee requires approval from the clinical faculty.
notes: Prepares students for direct practice across cognitive-behavioral, psychodynamic, and systems theoretical frameworks.
Training sites include the Center for Psychological Services, Douglass Developmental Disabilities Center, and community partnerships.
APA internship match required; students apply through APPIC in Year 4.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/clinical-department/clinical-psyd-overview

electives.description: One school psychology or professional psychology elective

year_5.label: Year 5 — APA Internship
year_5.note: Full-time APA-accredited predoctoral internship in a school or community setting.

dissertation_requirement.description: Original empirical or applied dissertation in school psychology. Committee includes GSAPP faculty.

practicum_sequence.description: Practicum begins Year 1; students are placed in schools and clinical settings each year, with increasing independence.

constraints: APA-accredited program; follows the Practitioner-Scholar model of training.
Students must complete comprehensive examinations before advancing to candidacy.
APA-accredited internship required in Year 5; students apply through APPIC.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required; no grade lower than B- accepted in practicum courses.
All dissertation requirements must be met before graduation.
School psychology licensure (NCSP or NJ Certification) obtained after program completion and supervision hours.
notes: Program emphasizes science-based assessment, intervention, consultation, and systems change within educational contexts.
Contact: Robin Hussey, Program Coordinator — rhussey@gsapp.rutgers.edu, (848) 445-3837.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/school-department/school-psyd-overview

fundamentals_of_psychology.description: Foundational psychology courses required of all doctoral students (6 credits)

core_foundational_op.description: Core foundational Organizational Psychology courses (15 credits)

core_stats_research_methods.description: Core Statistics and Research Methods (12 credits)

area_1_iwo_psychology.description: Area 1: Industrial, Work, and Organizational Psychology — choose 2 courses (6 credits)

area_2_op_od_practice.description: Area 2: OP/OD Practice — choose 2 courses (6 credits)

experiential_learning.description: Experiential Learning — internship or applied project (3 credits)

electives.description: Electives — choose 3 courses from departmental offerings (9 credits)

comprehensive_exam.description: Comprehensive examination required before advancement to doctoral candidacy. No separate credit assigned.

constraints: Total: 72 credits.
Comprehensive examination must be passed before beginning dissertation.
Students must select a dissertation advisor by the end of Year 2.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required throughout the program.
Internship (OPSY631) must be completed in an approved organizational setting.
Area 1 and Area 2 courses may overlap with Core courses — consult program advisor for approved selections.
Application deadline: January 31 for fall enrollment.
notes: Focuses on bridging psychological science and application to build high-performance, human-centered organizations.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/organizational-psychology-psyd-and-psym

core_foundational_op.description: Core foundational Organizational Psychology courses (12 credits)

core_stats_research_methods.description: Core Statistics and Research Methods (9 credits)

area_1_iwo_psychology.description: Area 1: Industrial, Work, and Organizational Psychology — choose 1 course (3 credits)

area_2_op_od_practice.description: Area 2: OP/OD Practice — choose 1 course (3 credits)

experiential_learning.description: Experiential Learning — internship, practicum, or applied project (3–6 credits)

constraints: Total: 42 credits.
No dissertation required; program is coursework and applied-learning focused.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Experiential learning can be 3 or 6 credits depending on project scope — consult advisor.
Application deadlines: March 1–May 1 for full review; late applications considered if space remains.
notes: The PsyM is a terminal master's degree intended for professionals seeking advanced organizational psychology training without a doctoral commitment.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/organizational-psychology-psyd-and-psym

practicum.description: Two semesters of supervised practicum experience at Rutgers ABA training sites (DDDC or RCAAS), meeting BACB supervised fieldwork requirements.

capstone_thesis_option.description: Students choose between a capstone or thesis route. Capstone consists of two courses (6 credits); thesis is a single 3-credit course plus independent research.

electives.description: Select elective ABA courses to complete 42 total credits. With capstone (6cr): 1 elective needed. With thesis (3cr): 2 electives needed.

bacb_eligibility.description: Coursework aligns with the BACB Fifth Edition Task List and meets BACB academic requirements for BCBA certification. Students must also complete supervised fieldwork hours and pass the BCBA examination.
notes: Completion of MABA alone does not guarantee BCBA certification eligibility.
Students should track fieldwork hours via the BACB Experience Tracker.
Practicum at DDDC and RCAAS counts toward supervised independent fieldwork hours.

training_sites: Douglass Developmental Disabilities Center (DDDC) — serving individuals with autism spectrum disorder across the lifespan
Rutgers Center for Adult Autism Services (RCAAS) — focused on adult ASD support and employment
constraints: Total: 42 credits (27 required core + 6 practicum + 6 capstone or 3 thesis + electives).
PPSY510 (Practicum I) requires concurrent enrollment with or prior completion of PPSY513.
PPSY521 (Ethics) is required and must be taken before advanced ABA courses.
Classes held evenings (3:35 p.m. or 6:15 p.m.) to accommodate working professionals.
All coursework is in-person.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Unique integration of didactic coursework with hands-on clinical training in nationally recognized ABA centers.
Thesis track is recommended for students considering doctoral study.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/applied-behavior-analysis-aba

practicum.description: Professional practicum placement in an approved clinical or organizational site (3 credits; may be repeated for additional credit)

capstone.description: Capstone Portfolio or Honors Capstone required for degree completion.

electives.description: Elective courses to complete the 42-credit requirement. Students may select from MAP, PPSY, CLPSY, SPSY, or OPSY offerings with advisor approval.

constraints: Total: 42 credits.
MAP505 (Practicum) requires placement arranged through GSAPP's office.
Capstone (MAP502 or MAP503) must be completed in the final semester.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Available at New Brunswick and Camden campuses.
Non-matriculated preview courses available: PPSY506 (Social Development Psychology) and MAP527 (Foundations of Psychotherapy).
notes: Integrates psychological science with applied training across clinical and professional contexts.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/master-applied-psychology

addictions_track_required.description: Required concentration courses for the Addictions Counseling Track (ACT)

practicum.description: Professional practicum placement in an addictions-focused setting (3 credits)

capstone.description: Capstone Portfolio or Honors Capstone required for degree completion.

electives.description: Additional elective courses to complete 42 credits.

constraints: Total: 42 credits.
MAP680 and MAP524 are required concentration courses unique to the ACT.
Practicum placement should be in an addictions-focused agency or clinical setting.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Graduates may be eligible for Licensed Clinical Alcohol and Drug Counselor (LCADC) pathway in NJ — verify with advisor.
notes: ACT prepares students for work in substance use disorder treatment, prevention, and policy settings.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/master-applied-psychology

map_mtss.major_name: Applied Psychology in Multi-Tiered Systems of Support (MAP-MTSS)

gse_required_courses.description: Two required courses from the Rutgers Graduate School of Education
notes: SPED523 — Teaching in Inclusive Classrooms (GSE 15:293:523, Spring, 3 credits)
GSE classroom organization course (15:293:TBD, Fall, 3 credits) — confirm with advisor for current offering

additional_required_courses.description: Additional MAP-MTSS required courses beyond certificate requirements
notes: MTSS in Secondary Educational Settings (18:820:540 — confirm current availability)
Multi-Tiered Social Emotional Learning and Behavioral Supports (18:820:TBD)
Restorative Practices and Psychological Wellness (18:820:TBD)

constraints: Total: 30 credits (18 certificate credits + 12 additional master's credits).
Fully online asynchronous format; students typically take 2 courses per semester.
PPSY533 requires a weekly virtual synchronous session with the instructor.
Prerequisites: bachelor's degree with coursework in education or psychology; teaching or school experience strongly preferred.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: The MAP-MTSS builds on the 18-credit Certificate in MTSS, adding deeper coursework in data-based decision making and school mental health.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/school-department/multi-tiered-systems-support-mtss

accelerated_41_map.major_name: Applied Psychology (MAP) — 4+1 Accelerated Bachelor's to Master's
accelerated_41_map.description: Accelerated program allowing Rutgers undergraduates to earn both a bachelor's degree and the 42-credit MAP degree in five years (4 undergraduate + 1 intensive master's year). Students begin taking graduate MAP courses in their senior year.

undergraduate_year_4_courses.description: Senior year: students take up to 12 graduate MAP credits that count toward both undergraduate electives and the MAP degree.
notes: Students typically take MAP507, MAP508, MAP506, and MAP527 during senior year.
Must maintain a minimum GPA of 3.0 and receive departmental approval to enroll.

graduate_year_courses.description: Fifth year (full-time master's year): students complete remaining 30 MAP credits, including practicum and capstone.

electives.notes: Complete remaining credits with MAP/PPSY/CLPSY electives — see MAP General Track for options.

constraints: Total MAP credits: 42 (12 taken as undergraduate senior + 30 in graduate year).
Admission requires a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 and completion of prerequisite courses in psychology.
Application submitted during junior year; acceptance to GSAPP required before senior MAP coursework begins.
Students officially become graduate students in their fifth year.
Practicum (MAP505) must be completed in the graduate year with approved placement.
notes: Designed for Rutgers undergraduates intending to pursue applied psychology careers.
Consult GSAPP admissions office for current participating undergraduate programs and application timeline.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/41-program-accelerated-bachelors-master-applied-psychology

certificate_aba.eligibility: Open to students with or currently pursuing a graduate degree. Courses meet BACB academic coursework requirements for BCBA certification eligibility.
certificate_aba.bacb_note: Certificate completion alone does not guarantee BCBA exam eligibility. Supervised fieldwork hours and exam passage are also required.
certificate_aba.format: In-person, evening classes (3:35 p.m. or 6:15 p.m., Monday–Thursday)
constraints: Students must hold or be pursuing a graduate degree to enroll.
Practicum hours toward BACB fieldwork may be arranged through GSAPP training sites (DDDC, RCAAS).
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
PPSY521 (Ethics) must be completed before advanced courses.
notes: Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/applied-behavior-analysis-aba

certificate_mtss.major_name: Multi-Tiered Systems of Support (MTSS) — Graduate Certificate

notes: SPED523 — Teaching in Inclusive Classrooms (GSE 15:293:523, Spring, 3 credits)
GSE Classroom Organization for Inclusive and Special Classrooms (15:293:TBD, Fall, 3 credits)

constraints: Total: 18 credits.
Fully online asynchronous; PPSY533 requires one hour of weekly synchronous virtual session.
Certificate serves as foundation for MAP-MTSS degree — all 18 credits apply toward the 30-credit master's.
Recommended for educators, school counselors, and school psychologists seeking data-driven intervention training.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/school-department/multi-tiered-systems-support-mtss

certificate_sport_perf_psych.major_name: Sport and Performance Psychology — Graduate Certificate

electives.description: Complete remaining credits from approved electives to reach 15 total. Director may approve other courses aligned with AASP requirements.
notes: EXSC455 (Exercise Psychology, undergraduate) may be approved by program director for students without equivalent background.

certificate_sport_perf_psych.eligibility: Open to graduate and qualified undergraduate students across disciplines including Kinesiology, Exercise Science, Athletic Training, Education, and Psychology. Graduate psychology students receive priority.
certificate_sport_perf_psych.aasp_alignment: Courses align with Association of Applied Sport Psychology (AASP) certification requirements.
constraints: Total: 15 credits (6 required + 9 electives).
GRE scores not required for admission.
Application requires transcripts, personal statement, and one letter of recommendation.
Program director must approve any non-listed elective for AASP credit.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Provides advanced training integrating sport and performance psychology into existing graduate or undergraduate studies.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/applied-department/sport-performance-psychology

certificate_suicide_prevention.major_name: Suicide Prevention, Practice, and Policy — Graduate Certificate
certificate_suicide_prevention.description: The first and only online certificate in the U.S. offered by a university focused exclusively on suicide prevention. Addresses economic, political, commercial, and social determinants of suicide at individual, community, and policy levels.
certificate_suicide_prevention.curriculum_note: Specific course codes are not yet published in the SIS catalog for this certificate. Contact GSAPP directly for the current course schedule and enrollment details.
constraints: Total: 12 credits.
100% online program.
Intended for mental health professionals, public health practitioners, policy makers, and researchers.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Program addresses suicide prevention from clinical practice, public health policy, and research perspectives.
Source: https://gsapp.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/certificates
